memoryArtifacts.headline: SMITH-SMYTHE FAMILY HISTORY FOLLOWS: 23 September 2019
memoryArtifacts.description:
ALEXANDER WHITE was born about 1550 of Sturton le Steeple, Nottinghamshire, England, to Thomas White (1524-1579) and Mary MacLean (1518-.)  He married Eleanor Smith.

Alexander White died 6 May 1596, …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Eley Smythe於1460年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾維爾布時，他的父親William Richard Smythe48歲，母親Mary Margaret Cromwell42歲。他於1479年與Lady Anne le Strange在英國林可爾史爾武特比結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1543年4月在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾享年83歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可武斯布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Eley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Helen , now also used outside Wales. It is identical with the Welsh vocabulary word elen ‘nymph’, but this is unlikely to be the origin. It is found in Welsh texts from an early period as an equivalent of Helen, for example as the name of the mother of Constantine, finder of the True Cross.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Eley Smythe was born in 1460, in Welbourn, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Richard Smythe, was 48 and his mother, Mary Margaret Cromwell, was 42. He married Lady Anne le Strange in 1479, in Utterby, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died in April 1543, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83, and was buried in Uxbridge, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Lady Anne le Strange en 1479, à Utterby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en avril 1543, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à Uxbridge, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Eley Smytheが1460年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウエルボウルンで生まれた時，父William Richard Smytheは48歳，母Mary Margaret Cromwellは42歳であった。1479年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウッテルビーでLady Anne le Strangeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1543年4月にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアで83歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウクスブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Eley Smythe(이)가 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 웰보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Richard Smythe(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Margaret Cromwell(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1479년 영국 링코실르 우터비에서 Lady Anne le Strange(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1543년 4월 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 스브리지에 묻혔다.

ru.Carlton on Trent, Nottinghamshire, Engla: карлтон он трент, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de Eley Smythe родился в 1460 г. в местности велбурн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Richard Smythe было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Margaret Cromwell, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne le Strange в 1479 г. в местности уттирби, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер апрель 1543 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности уксбридж, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de Eley Smythe nació en 1460, en Welbourn, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Richard Smythe, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Mary Margaret Cromwell, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Lady Anne le Strange en 1479, en Utterby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en abril de 1543, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en Uxbridge, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
